20030414_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Blue-Gre.description: Blue-Green Threshold: cheap efficient beaters like Wild Mongrel and Nimble Mongoose, backed by Circular Logic and graveyard-fueled card advantage once threshold turns on.

20030421_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Dark_Con.description: Rogue lock deck: Solitary Confinement draws free cards while Millstone and Compulsion grind the opponent's library away for a control-combo win.

20030428_Building_On_A_Budget_-_ElfBall.description: Elf tribal combo generating huge mana off Llanowar Elves and friends to fuel Aggravated Assault loops or Fireball-style finishers.

20030505_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Red-Gree.description: Red-Green burn/aggro pairing efficient beaters like Wild Mongrel with direct-damage spells such as Firebolt and Volcanic Hammer for a fast clock.

20030520_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Recycled.description: Zombie tribal midrange that can beat down, wrath the board, or grind, powered by Patriarch's Bidding and Nantuko Husk sacrifice synergies.

20030527_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Goblins.description: Red-black Goblin tribal aggro leaning on Goblin Sledder and Sharpshooter sacrifice synergies to clear blockers and race the opponent down.

20030602_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Slivery_.description: Sliver tribal deck stacking evasion, poison, and toughness-boosting keyword Slivers into a resilient, synergy-driven creature swarm.

20030609_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Squirrel.description: Squirrel Prison combo: Opposition and Squirrel Nest churn out squirrels to enable a Static Orb lock that leaves opponents permanently tapped down.

20030616_Building_On_A_Budget_Burning_Bl.description: Blue-red tempo-burn deck using Merfolk Looter and card draw to fuel Grim Lavamancer and a suite of efficient direct-damage spells.

20030623_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Clerics.description: White-black Cleric tribal built around fun combos like Pariah plus Daunting Defender to stonewall attackers before grinding out a win.

20030630_Building_on_a_Budget_-_Beasty_B.description: Green Beast tribal midrange casting oversized creatures like Krosan Tusker and Krosan Warchief to simply out-size the opponent.

20030708_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Ring_The.description: Onslaught-block morph deck ambushing opponents with Carrion Feeder and surprise unmorph value plays for repeatable card advantage.

20030714_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Reanimat.description: Classic Reanimator: discard huge threats like Akroma and Visara with Buried Alive or Last Rites, then bring them back for a fraction of the cost.

20030721_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Mutants.description: Mutant tribal midrange leaning on landcycling creatures like Twisted Abomination to smooth draws while building toward a Patriarch's Bidding payoff.

20030818_Building_On_A_Budget_-_The_Claw.description: Ramp deck accelerating with Explosive Vegetation into hard-hitting dragons like Rorix Bladewing and Imperial Hellkite for a fast finish.

20030909_Building_on_a_Budget_-_Husky_Ca.description: Onslaught-block combo aggro turning Nantuko Husk into a 20/20 attacker alongside Caller of the Claw and Wirewood Elf synergies.

20030915_Building_On_A_Budget_-_TurboRhy.description: Turbo-Biorhythm combo: land destruction and mana acceleration stall opponents while building enough creature power to end the game instantly.

20030929_Building_on_a_Budget_-_For_The_.description: Bird tribal aggro swarming the skies with Battle Screech tokens and evasive fliers like Skywing Aven and Wingbeat Warrior.

20031006_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Druids.description: Druid tribal ramp deck chaining mana dorks and Collective Unconscious card draw toward a giant Hurricane or Kamahl finish.

20031013_Building_On_A_Budget_-_In_The_A.description: White Weenie Soldier tribal aggro, curving out small efficient troops backed by a few bigger finishers for the late game.

20031020_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Budget_B.description: A primer Beatdown deck: cheap threats and burn like Firebolt racing the opponent down before they can stabilize the board.

20031027_Building_on_a_Budget_-_Budget_C.description: A primer Control deck built on Counterspell-style answers and a Psychatog finisher, grinding to card advantage before closing the game.

20031103_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Aggro-Co.description: A primer Aggro-Control deck: midrange threats and looters like Wild Mongrel bridging the gap between pure Beatdown and pure Control.

20031110_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Rogue.description: A Rogue land-destruction deck wiping mana with Obliterate while Pyroclasm and burn spells finish off opponents who can't rebuild.

20031117_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Combo.description: A primer Combo deck chaining Elf and morph creatures with Caller of the Claw into a repeatable value or kill engine.

20031124_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Affinity.description: Mirrodin Affinity aggro: cheap artifact creatures like Frogmite and Myr Enforcer cast almost for free, backed by Thirst for Knowledge card draw.

20031201_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Wallchuc.description: Limited-turned-Constructed combo: Wall of Blood's stored damage triggers a monstrous Grab the Reins attack for a surprise one-shot kill.

20031208_Building_On_A_Budget_-_You-Con-.description: Toolbox combo pairing Power Conduit's counters with Decree of Silence to lock opponents out of casting spells while assembling a win.

20031215_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Wild_Des.description: Budget Storm combo untapping mana with Twiddle and Wild Growth to chain cheap spells into Mind's Desire and a lethal Tendrils of Agony.

20031222_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Holiday_.description: Urza-land ramp into Bosh, Iron Golem, sacrificing artifacts for direct damage while Mindslaver locks opponents out of their own turns.

20040105_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Vaultato.description: Affinity-based combo aggro feeding artifacts to Atog and Myr Incubator while Disciple of the Vault converts sacrifices into direct damage.

20040112_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Dark_Sun.description: Mono-black control grinding with Icy Manipulator and Barter in Blood before draining the opponent out with Consume Spirit.

20040119_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Gunner.description: Chaotic Orcish Artillery deck paired with Spirit Link, turning random damage into a life-gain engine alongside aggressive Goblins.

20040126_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Prepare_.description: Red aggro built around the triple-red War Elemental beatstick, backed by Shock and Volcanic Hammer-style burn to close games fast.

20040202_Building_On_A_Budget_-_Robot_Ni.description: Blue control inspired by Counter-Post: cheap affinity threats like Frogmite free up mana for Mana Leak and card-drawing counterspell backup.

20040213_Viva_la_magicthegatheringcom.description: White-black Cleric tribal using Rotlung Reanimator and Scion of Darkness to convert dying creatures into a growing token army.

20040220_Scepter-cize.description: Isochron Scepter lock deck imprinting Holy Day or Boomerang to repeatedly stonewall the opponent while assembling an inevitable win.

20040227_Sleight-Knight.description: Knight tribal using Mind Bend to rewrite Circles of Protection and Karma on the fly, turning the opponent's own hosers into blowouts.

20040305_Cowardice.description: Cowardice bounce-lock control: cheap Affinity threats bounce the opponent's creatures every turn while you rebuild your board faster than they can.

20040312_Elf-Clamp.description: Elf tribal aggro using Skullclamp on expendable 1-toughness Elves for a relentless card-advantage engine backing the beatdown.

20040319_Iron_Man.description: Indestructible artifact army animated by March of the Machines, turning Darksteel permanents into an unkillable robot horde.

20040326_Beatstick.description: Proteus Staff combo: cheap token generators like Raise the Alarm get flipped into a game-ending Darksteel Colossus.

20040402_Myr_Engineering.description: Artifact ramp deck using Vedalken Engineer to cheat expensive Mirrodin artifacts into play several turns ahead of schedule.

20040409_Scorpion_Foundry.description: Infinite combo grinding Myr Moonvessel through Soul Foundry and Dross Scorpion for repeatable mana and damage until the opponent falls.

20040416_Rootshaper.description: A leaner, cheaper combo engine cycling Myr Moonvessel through Soul Foundry for mana, closing games out with a giant Fireball.

20040423_Sorcerer.description: Black-red sorcery-based control trading removal like Pyroclasm and Sever Soul for life and cards before finishing with burn or a Demon token.

20040430_Scorched_Earth.description: Land-destruction control mixing Molten Rain and Stone Rain with creature removal to strangle the opponent's resources before they can recover.

20040507_Weird_Puzzle.description: Zur's Weirding lock deck vetoing opponents' draw steps while Teferi's Puzzle Box and card advantage keep you ahead of the stall.

20040514_MustAttack.description: Juggernaut-centered aggro deck pumping an unstoppable attacker with tricks like Savage Beating and Predator's Strike for a fast clock.

20040521_Reflections.description: Panoptic Mirror control repeatedly imprinting Plow Under to bury the opponent's lands while removal like Nekrataal clears the board.

20040528_Bad_Eggs.description: Cantrip-egg combo deck cracking a swarm of mana-fixing artifact 'cogs' to fuel Disciple of the Vault and Nantuko Shrine damage.

20040604_Elf_Assault.description: Elf tribal splashing Seismic Assault to turn extra lands from Sprouting Vines into a steady stream of direct damage.

20040611_Men_of_Mana.description: Myr tribal ramp: a swarm of mana-producing Myr and Urza lands power out early threats, with Myr Matrix soaking up excess mana.

20040618_Vedalken_Winds.description: Reader-submitted combo bouncing permanents with Vedalken Archmage and Words of Wind until the opponent is left with nothing to do.

20040625_Offering_of_a_Lifetime.description: Green-black sacrifice deck feeding Elves and tokens to Death Pit Offering for a game-ending army-wide power pump.

20040702_Dream_Droplets.description: Lifegain control chaining Sun Droplet counters into Well of Lost Dreams card draw, out-lasting aggression while refueling every turn.

20040709_Demonic_Hordes.description: Mono-black Demon midrange ramping into oversized threats like Reiver Demon and Lord of the Pit for a punishing late game.

20040716_Bauble_Masters.description: White-blue artifact toolbox recurring cheap 'baubles' with Auriok Salvagers and Artificer's Intuition to dig up whatever answer you need.

20040723_Red_Head.description: Mono-red burn nostalgia deck, curving efficient beaters and direct damage like Magma Jet toward a game-ending Fireball.

20040730_Disruption_Beats.description: Black disruption-beatdown pairing fast, cheap threats with hand disruption like Blackmail and removal to clear a path to the opponent's face.

20040806_LifeBurst.description: Sunburst lifegain deck out-healing the opponent's damage with Sun Droplet and Clearwater Goblet before grinding them down with Infused Arrows.

20040813_Pristine_Green.description: Three-color control drawing cards and removing threats before closing with a game-ending Rude Awakening or an unblockable Pristine Angel.

20040820_Spikeshots_Cap.description: Affinity-style equipment aggro turning a cheap Spikeshot Goblin into a massive pinging threat once Cranial Plating comes online.

20040827_Fates_Entwined.description: Black control disrupting the opponent with discard and removal while the novel Shared Fate lets you borrow their own deck.

20040903_Horobis_Army.description: White weenie aggro curving efficient small creatures like Savannah Lions toward a free preview-card bomb finisher.

20040910_Ironworks.description: Krark-Clan Ironworks combo dumping cheap artifacts into play to fuel a Goblin Cannon or Goblin Charbelcher kill.

20040917_Catching_the_Low-cost_Blues.description: A no-frills mono-blue counterspell control deck stalling with fifteen counterspells before winning slowly with Vedalken Shackles or Stalking Stones.

20040924_Its_Elementary_my_Dear_Watson.description: Land-recursion value deck turning extra lands into Elemental creatures via Wand of the Elements, powered by Crucible of Worlds.

20041001_Common_Combo.description: An all-common Storm-style combo untapping mana with Twiddle and Fertile Ground to chain cantrips into a Temporal Fissure lock.

20041008_Light_em_up.description: Mono-red burn deck imprinting a cheap burn spell on Isochron Scepter for a repeatable direct-damage engine.

20041015_Beacon_Blaster.description: Beacon of Creation token engine sacrificing Saprolings through Blasting Station while Fecundity refills your hand for a grinding win.

20041022_Winning_Big.description: Big-mana ramp deck casting Tooth and Nail to fetch Sundering Titan and Darksteel Colossus for an overwhelming finish.

20041105_Law_of_the_Sword.description: White Samurai tribal aggro equipping efficient bushido creatures with Bonesplitter and Grafted Wargear to push through early damage.

20041112_Building_on_a_Budget_Comes_Unhi.description: A tongue-in-cheek Unhinged-themed budget deck: efficient black-red aggro with cheap beaters, discard, and hard removal like Terminate.

20041119_The_Warriors_Call.description: Green-red Warrior tribal aggro pumping a wide board of Kamigawa Warriors with tricks like Kodama's Might for explosive attacks.

20041126_The_Warriors_Call.description: (Holiday rerun) Green-red Warrior tribal aggro pumping a wide board of Kamigawa Warriors with tricks like Kodama's Might for explosive attacks.

20041203_Building_on_a_Budget_Sleighty_F.description: Blue-white Sleight Knight aggro-combo swapping Circles of Protection and Karma's named color to blank removal while Knights and Swords finish the game.

20041210_Shrines_that_Work.description: Five-color Honden shrine deck stacking legendary enchantment triggers each upkeep for snowballing card, life, and damage value.

20041217_Family_of_Serpents.description: Snake tribal deck growing counters and evasive threats with Seshiro the Anointed synergies before closing with a big Fireball.

20041224_Fates_Entwined.description: (Reprint) Black control disrupting the opponent with discard and removal while the novel Shared Fate lets you borrow their own deck.

20041231_Common_Combo.description: (Reprint) An all-common Storm-style combo untapping mana with Twiddle and Fertile Ground to chain cantrips into a Temporal Fissure lock.

20050107_Rats_Without_Honor.description: Rat tribal aggro-discard, stripping the opponent's hand with Chittering Rats and Nezumi Cutthroat while a swarm of Rats closes the game.

20050114_Silent_Stick.description: Arcane/Splice combo assembling a three-card engine through Peer Through Depths and Sift Through Sands to cheat out The Unspeakable early.

20050121_Soilcraft.description: Green-red Spirit synergy deck combining Soilshaper and Kodama of the South Tree with burn spells for a fast, damage-doubling clock.

20050128_The_Dark_Side.description: Black Spirit-discard aggro built around Jade Idol, an indestructible recurring threat that dodges most board wipes.

20050204_Colorful_Zuberas.description: Zubera tribal sacrifice deck mass-sacrificing the whole cycle at once with Devouring Greed or Barter in Blood for a burst of damage or life loss.

20050211_Wizards_of_the_Moon.description: Wizard tribal control drawing piles of cards off Azami, Lady of Scrolls before closing with Meloku the Clouded Mirror's token army.

20050218_Heartbeat_of_Legend.description: Legend-ramp deck doubling mana with Heartbeat of Spring to cheat out powerful Kamigawa legends far ahead of curve.

20050225_Red_in_the_East.description: Mono-red burn-aggro leaning on Zo-Zu the Punisher and Lava Spike-style burn to close out games before the opponent stabilizes.

20050304_Charge_it.description: Charge-counter artifact toolbox using Thunderstaff and Sculpting Steel to lock down creatures while synergies like Lightning Coils grind out a win.

20050311_Oni-potent.description: Black-red Ogre and Demon midrange casting punishing, high-risk beaters like Bloodthirsty Ogre and Yukora for a fast, painful clock.

20050318_Secret_Sword.description: Blue-black Ninja tribal sneaking in Ninjutsu creatures like Ink-Eyes, Servant of Oni for repeated hand disruption and evasive damage.

20050321_Ninjutsu_From_Samurai_to_Ninja.description: First step evolving a Kamigawa precon into a budget Ninja deck, bouncing and rebuying Ninjutsu triggers for repeated value.

20050328_Ninjutsu_Talons_Truth_and_Throw.description: Second installment refining the budget Ninja precon, tightening the mana base and adding more Ninjutsu triggers for consistency.

20050404_Ninjutsu_Free_To_Be_You_And_Me.description: Final installment of the budget Ninja precon evolution, adding disruption and card selection to finish a lean, evasive Ninjutsu deck.

20050411_Interlude_Hidden_Gems_in_Betray.description: An exploration of overlooked 'junk rare' build-around cards from Betrayers of Kamigawa, spotlighting under-the-radar combo and synergy pieces.

20050418_Dark_Devotion_Of_Hunger_and_Thi.description: First step evolving a black-red Ogre/Demon precon, trimming weak cards while keeping its aggressive, high-risk-high-reward creatures.

20050425_Dark_Devotion_Critters_and_Crit.description: Second installment of the Ogre/Demon precon evolution, adding removal and refining the creature base for better consistency.

20050502_Dark_Devotion_A_Demonic_Epic.description: Final Ogre/Demon precon evolution topping the curve with Kuro, Pitlord and Zombify recursion for a punishing black-red midrange finish.

20050509_Interlude_Kamigawas_Commons_Rev.description: A survey of the best (and worst) Champions/Betrayers of Kamigawa commons worth stocking for budget deckbuilding.

20050516_Rats_Nest_Ratimation.description: Kickoff of a Rat tribal precon evolution, arming a swarm of Nezumi with Umezawa's Jitte before shifting toward a reanimation sub-theme.

20050523_Rats_Nest_Jitte_Versus_The_Quee.description: Second step of the Rat precon evolution, trading the Jitte-weenie plan for a Nezumi reanimation theme with Zombify and Okiba-Gang Shinobi.

20050530_Rats_Nest_Jitte_Versus_The_Quee.description: (Holiday rerun) Second step of the Rat precon evolution, trading the Jitte-weenie plan for a Nezumi reanimation theme with Zombify.

20050606_Rats_Nest_The_Many_Flavors_of_R.description: Final Ratimation build: black Rat tribal blending discard, Ninjutsu, and Zombify reanimation into a lean, aggressive attrition deck.

20050613_Interlude_No_Birds_No_Problem.description: A survey of budget mana-acceleration and fixing options for green decks who can't afford Birds of Paradise.

20050620_Interlude_Standardizing_Samurai.description: Updating the earlier Samurai Fish precon-turned-deck for the current Standard format, a white-blue Samurai aggro-tempo build.

20050627_Interlude_BOAB_Smackdown.description: A four-deck round robin pitting the column's finished precon evolutions -- Ratimation, Samurai Fish, Club Ninja, and Bad Religion -- against each other.

20050704_Interlude_BOAB_Smackdown.description: (Holiday rerun) A four-deck round robin pitting the column's finished precon evolutions against each other for bragging rights.

20050711_Spirit_Flames_Of_Kannushi_and_G.description: Kickoff of a green-red Spirit tribal precon evolution, chaining Arcane spells through Splice and soulshift Spirits for snowballing value.

20050718_Spirit_Flames_The_Allure_of_Lur.description: Second step of the Spirit Flames evolution, tuning the green-red Spirit deck for Kamigawa Block with more consistent mana.

20050725_Spirit_Flames_Hana_Kami_Freedom.description: Final Spirit Flames build ('Soul Train'): a green-red Spirit swarm using Unchecked Growth and Manriki-Gusari for reach and ramp.

20050801_Interlude_The_Budget_Saviors.description: Wrapping up and naming the finished green-red Spirit deck 'Soul Train' after a reader naming contest.

20050808_Blood_Clock_Five_Bloody_Clocks.description: Kickoff of a Blood Clock precon evolution, exploring which color pairs best with the discard-based artifact engine.

20050815_Blood_Clock_Damnation_In_The_Ba.description: Black Blood Clock deck stripping the opponent's hand with Waking Nightmare and Kiku's Shadow while grinding out card advantage.

20050822_Blood_Clock_Tool_Time.description: Final Blood Clock build splashing green ramp into the black discard shell for smoother mana and a faster clock.

20050829_Interlude_Investing_in_Real_Est.description: A case for budget players to invest in Ninth Edition's rare painlands despite the cost, as essential long-term mana fixing.

20050905_Interlude_Investing_in_Real_Est.description: (Holiday rerun) A case for budget players to invest in Ninth Edition's rare painlands despite the cost, as essential mana fixing.

20050912_DeadAflame_A_Home_For_Zombify.description: Mashing two Ninth Edition precons into a black-red deck blending Zombify reanimation with burn sorceries like Wildfire.

20050919_DeadAflame_Beware_The_Husk.description: Second step of the Dead/Aflame evolution, adding Nantuko Husk sacrifice synergy alongside Zombify reanimation and burn.

20050926_DeadAflame_Death_Sorceries_and_.description: Final Dead/Aflame build: a lean black-red reanimation and sacrifice deck using Cruel Edict and Threaten to close out games.

20051003_Interlude_My_Mirage_Precon_Part.description: Behind-the-scenes look at designing an official Mirage preconstructed deck, weighing options like a Green/Red fatties build.

20051010_Interlude_My_Mirage_Precon_Part.description: Second half of designing an official Mirage precon: a black 'Nightstalkers' shell of evasive shadow creatures and discard.

20051024_Interlude_Decks_That_Werent.description: A grab-bag of pet-deck ideas and rogue combo pieces reconsidered for viability after Ravnica rotated Standard around them.

20051031_Selesnya_United_Tokens_On_My_Ta.description: Kickoff of a Selesnya precon evolution, building a green-white token-and-convoke shell around Scatter the Seeds and Siege Wurm.

20051107_Selesnya_United_The_Emperors_Ne.description: Second step of the Selesnya token evolution, trimming the curve and adding Watchwolf and Llanowar Elves for consistency.

20051114_Selesnya_United_The_Token_Beats.description: Final Selesnya build: a dedicated green-white beatdown deck around Emperor Crocodile and a wide token board from Scatter the Seeds.

20051121_Interlude_The_Budget_Selesnya_C.description: A staples guide to budget Selesnya (green-white) cards worth stocking across Ravnica-era Standard.

20051128_Followed_Footsteps_Dimirs_Pitte.description: Kickoff of a House Dimir precon evolution built around Followed Footsteps, cloning creatures for repeatable value or a combo finish.

20051205_Followed_Footsteps_Gasps_Guards.description: Second step of the Dimir evolution, balancing Ninja, Spirit, and reanimation sub-themes around the Followed Footsteps clone engine.

20051212_Followed_Footsteps_Transmutasti.description: Final Followed Footsteps build: a blue-black transmute toolbox tutoring for exactly the answer or clone target needed.

20051219_Interlude_My_Mirage_Precon_Part.description: (Retrospective) Designing an official Mirage precon: a black 'Nightstalkers' shell of evasive shadow creatures and discard.

20051226_Interlude_BOAB_Smackdown.description: (Retrospective) A four-deck round robin pitting the column's finished precon evolutions against each other for bragging rights.

20060102_Interlude_Investing_in_Real_Est.description: (Retrospective) A case for budget players to invest in Ninth Edition's rare painlands despite the cost, as essential mana fixing.

20060109_Interlude_The_Budget_House_Dimi.description: A staples guide to budget House Dimir (blue-black) cards spanning beatdown, control, and sneaky combo strategies.

20060116_Interlude_BOAB_Smackdown_II.description: A five-deck round robin pitting the column's latest precon evolutions against each other in a bracket for bragging rights.

20060123_Drafting_Golgari_Staring_At_Ins.description: Kickoff evolving a drafted Golgari deck into Standard, built around the Mortipede plus Gaze of the Gorgon graveyard-recursion interaction.

20060130_Drafting_Golgari_Morty_Meets_Mo.description: Second step of the Golgari evolution, tuning the black-green graveyard-recursion shell with more Mortipede synergy pieces.

20060206_Drafting_Golgari_Relaxing_Putre.description: Final Golgari build adding Putrefy removal and Svogthos, the Restless Tomb as a resilient graveyard-fueled threat.

20060213_Interlude_Spare-Time_Decks.description: A grab-bag of pet decks the author's been tinkering with in spare time, offered as launching pads for your own ideas.

20060220_Interlude_The_Budget_Golgari.description: A staples guide to budget Golgari (black-green) cards for graveyard-value and grinding midrange strategies.

20060227_Charge_of_the_Boros_Swift_And_T.description: Kickoff of a Boros precon evolution, a red-white aggro deck leaning on Skyknight Legionnaire and combat tricks.

20060306_Charge_Of_The_Boros_Concerted_S.description: Second step of the Boros evolution, adding double-strike creatures and equipment for a harder-hitting red-white aggro curve.

20060320_Charge_Of_The_Boros_A_Boros_Bye.description: Final Boros build and the author's farewell column: a lean red-white aggro deck topped off with Vulshok Morningstar and Sunforger.

20060403_Discard_This_Deck.description: Debut column from a new author: a blue-black discard-and-mill control deck grinding out card advantage with Compulsive Research and Megrim.

20060410_Gruul_The_Wilding_Part_1.description: Kickoff evolving the Gruul Wilding precon into a Standard deck, a green-red midrange-aggro shell of big creatures and pump spells.

20060417_Gruul_The_Wilding_Part_2.description: Final Gruul Wilding build: a green-red beatdown deck topping the curve with Borborygmos and Bramble Elemental.

20060424_Exploding_Wurms_Part_1.description: Grixis combo burning the opponent out with Undying Flames plus a discarded Autochthon Wurm, backed by Zombify and dredge synergy.

20060501_Exploding_Wurms_Part_2.description: Final Exploding Wurms build refining the Undying Flames/Autochthon Wurm burn combo with better mana and more consistency.

20060508_I_Heart_Sky_Swallower.description: A tongue-in-cheek reader-poll deck nominally about Sky Swallower, really a blue-black control shell with Zur's Weirding and hand disruption.

20060515_Better_Deckbuilding_101_Underst.description: A theory-focused article explaining Aggro, Combo, Control, and Tempo archetypes, illustrated with a white-black 'Ghost Dad' tempo example.

20060522_Grand_Spree.description: Mycosynth Lattice combo turning every permanent into an artifact, then blasting the board with Shattering Spree and Shrapnel Blast.

20060529_Grand_Spree.description: (Holiday rerun) Mycosynth Lattice combo turning every permanent into an artifact, then blasting the board with Shattering Spree.

20060605_Deckbuilding_101_Five_Tips_for_.description: A deckbuilding-theory column outlining five common pitfalls, illustrated with examples spanning nearly every archetype and color.

20060612_Deck_Doctor_Izzet_Pinball_Part_.description: Deck-doctoring a reader's blue-red pinger deck built around Curiosity plus Niv-Mizzet and a Quicksilver Dagger-enchanted Dwarven Patrol.

20060619_Deck_Doctor_Izzet_Pinball_Part_.description: Second half of tuning the Izzet Pinball deck, trimming the manabase and card choices for a more consistent ping-damage engine.

20060626_Azorius_Ascendant_The_Prelude.description: Kickoff evolving the Azorius theme deck, exploring blue-white control's classic card-advantage and board-control roots.

20060703_Battle_of_Wits_Budget_Dissensio.description: A 200+ card Battle of Wits build using color-fixing Signets and cantrips to reliably assemble the 250-card win condition.

20060710_Azorius_Ascendant_Control_Part_.description: First control-focused step of the Azorius evolution, explaining card advantage as control's core building block.

20060717_Azorius_Ascendant_Thank_you_Sir.description: Second step refining the blue-white Azorius control deck, answering reader questions about card choices and curve.

20060724_Azorius_Ascendant_One_Piece_At_.description: Final Azorius Ascendant build: a blue-white control deck focused on board control, card advantage, and a late-game finisher.

20060731_Stealing_My_Muse.description: A blue control deck built around Muse Vessel, stealing and casting spells straight out of the opponent's own hand.

20060807_Deck_Doctor_Sasaya_My_Dreams_Pa.description: Deck-doctoring a reader's Sasaya, Orochi Ascendant ramp deck, diagnosing fundamental mana and consistency problems.

20060814_Wrecking_with_Rakdos_Part_1_of_.description: Kickoff of a Rakdos theme-deck evolution, a black-red sacrifice-and-burn aggro shell built around Lyzolda, the Blood Witch.

20060821_Deck_Doctor_Sasaya_My_Dreams_Pa.description: Final Sasaya deck-doctoring pass, trimming the mana engine and adding Life from the Loam recursion for a smoother ramp plan.

20060828_Wrecking_with_Rakdos_Part_2_of_.description: Second step of the Rakdos evolution, pushing toward Hellbent aggro that rewards emptying your hand for bonus effects.

20060904_Wrecking_with_Rakdos_Part_2_of_.description: (Holiday rerun) Second step of the Rakdos evolution, pushing toward Hellbent aggro that rewards emptying your hand.

20060911_Wrecking_With_Rakdos_Part_3_of_.description: Final Rakdos build pivoting from Hellbent aggro to Hellbent control, grinding with removal and Lyzolda's sacrifice synergy.

20060918_Living_a_Double_Life_Part_1_of_.description: A tongue-in-cheek reveal of an old rare-heavy Standard control deck, built around Wrath of God and Beacon of Immortality lifegain.

20060925_Living_a_Double_Life_Life_Suppo.description: Deck-doctoring a reader's Beacon of Immortality combo deck that buys time before killing with Goblin Charbelcher or False Cure.

20061002_Living_a_Double_Life_Eight_Mana.description: Final Double Life build: an Orzhov control shell using Beacon of Immortality lifegain to buy time for a False Cure kill.

20061009_Blue_Snow_Aggro.description: A Coldsnap 'junk rare' build using snow-matters payoffs like Rimewind Taskmage alongside Ninjutsu creatures for tempo-aggro damage.

20061016_Battle_of_Wits_Minus_100_Degree.description: Updating the 250-card Battle of Wits deck with Coldsnap silver-bullet singletons, leaning on transmute cards as pseudo-tutors.

20061023_Battle_of_Wits_Deep_Freeze_Part.description: Final Battle of Wits update, shifting to a one-of-heavy 'silver bullet' strategy that the deck's massive size can reliably support.

20061030_There_Can_Only_Be_Fun_Five-Colo.description: A Halloween gimmick five-color Highlander-style Standard deck packing one of nearly everything for maximum variety.

20061106_Whats_Upton.description: Black-red Extended control mixing artifact creatures like Triskelion with Persecute discard and sweepers like Starstorm and Oblivion Stone.

20061113_The_Evolution_of_Fractured_Crus.description: A mashup evolution combining four Time Spiral theme decks -- Fungus, Slivers, suspend-based Reality Fracture, and white weenie Crusaders.

20061120_Wait_for_it_Reality_Fracture_Pa.description: Evolving the suspend-themed Reality Fracture deck into a blue-red build mixing Ignite Memories storm with Rift Bolt-style burn.

20061127_Double_Mayhem.description: A guest return from Nate Heiss: blue-green counters-matter deck doubling growth with Doubling Season and Spike-cycle creatures.

20061204_Burning_with_Suspense_Reality_F.description: Final Reality Fracture build combining suspend creatures and Grapeshot-style storm for an aggressive blue-red suspend-burn deck.

20061211_Eight-Post.description: Extended Cloudpost ramp deck accelerating into Mindslaver locks and Oblivion Stone board wipes for total board control.

20061218_Deckbuilding_101_Five_Tips_for_.description: (Retrospective) A deckbuilding-theory column outlining five common pitfalls, illustrated with examples across nearly every archetype.

20061225_Stealing_My_Muse.description: (Retrospective) A blue control deck built around Muse Vessel, stealing and casting spells straight out of the opponent's own hand.

20070101_Eight-Post.description: (Retrospective) Extended Cloudpost ramp deck accelerating into Mindslaver locks and Oblivion Stone board wipes for total control.

20070115_Fun_With_Fungus_Part_1.description: Kickoff evolving the Fun With Fungus theme deck, a green-black Thallid/Saproling token swarm feeding sacrifice payoffs.

20070122_Fun_With_Fungus_Part_2_of_2.description: Final Fun With Fungus build, fixing the black-green mana base and adding Thelonite Hermit and Putrefy for a stronger finish.

20070129_Battle_of_Wits_Now_with_100_Mor.description: Fourth update to the 240+ card Battle of Wits deck, folding in new Time Spiral tutors and cantrips for the win condition.

20070205_Booby_Trapped.description: Blue-red combo pairing Mishra, Artificer Prodigy with Booby Trap for a surprise burst of direct damage off cheap artifacts.

20070212_Back_to_the_Rak_Part_1_of_2.description: Revisiting the black-red Rakdos control shell with new Time Spiral tools like Jaya Ballard for a tournament-ready update.

20070219_Back_to_the_Rak_Sideboardin_Tim.description: Finishing the Rakdos control update with a full sideboard plan as the deck graduates to the competitive tournament room.

20070226_Building_On_No_Budget_2.description: A joke column about a robot's 'perfectly budget' mono-Forest deck, with the real decklist a lean white weenie tempo build.

20070305_10_Decks_in_10_Weeks_The_Two_La.description: Week 1 of a 10-week color-swap experiment: a green-white Enchantress deck built around Mesa Enchantress and Verduran Enchantress value.

20070430_10_Decks_in_10_Weeks_Profusion_.description: Week 9 swaps to black-green for a ramp-and-attrition deck combining Smallpox disruption with Rampant Growth mana acceleration.

20070507_10_Decks_in_10_Weeks_Fungal_Beh.description: Final week of the color-swap experiment: a green-blue ramp deck closing out with the massive Fungal Behemoth.

20070514_Battle_of_Wits_con_Planar_Chaos.description: Fifth update to the mega Battle of Wits deck, adding Planar Chaos tutors and mana-fixing for a smoother 250-card build.
